title: Export rootfs of an image
body:
It'd be nice if buildah could export a rootfs to a directory, tarball, or mount to a given directory. My use case is that I'd like to use `systemd-nspawn` to run the resulting containers, but there's no way to export and/or mount an image filesystem at a well-known location (namely `/var/lib/machines/$nspawn_name`) for use with it.

I suppose one way to do it is to have some `ExecStartPre=` to `buildah mount` followed by a bind mount to `/var/lib/machines/%i`, but passing the directory that `buildah` has mounted needs a pipe which isn't easy without a shell script or the like to make the proper command line. It would also be susceptible to `buildah umount -a` causing failures (either on the `systemd-nspawn` side or on the `buildah umount` side).

comments.body: Actually, I missed that only *containers* can be mounted, so `buildah mount $image` would be nice to have first.
A quick poke makes it look like it isn't exactly straightforward, but I also might be missing something.

Do you want this thing to be readonly?  If readonly we could allow the mounting of an image, but it would take some wiring into containers/storage and then be pickupable by podman/buildah.

If you want to be able to modify the image, then you really do want a container.  A buildah container that has not been modified is pretty much the equivalent of an image.
My containers are `ReadOnly=true`, so I'm fine with that restriction.

@nalind How hard would it be to get containers/storage to mount images read/only at a specific location?  Or would we still need to mount then in the same location, and force the user to bind mount to their desired location?
I think we will tell users to bind mount from the hard coded location.

That's unfortunate because that location is not stable (it depends on the container ID which is not predictable given an image name/ID). Maybe some kind of "how to" example could be added to the docs?

@mathstuf See example
```
crossdev_container=$(buildah from "$DOCKER_USERNAME/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-gentoo-stable-amd64-crossdev:latest")
crossdev=$(buildah mount "$crossdev_container")
buildah copy "$container" "$crossdev/usr/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/" /
buildah unmount "$crossdev_container"
```

I've just invented this code, works fine.

I ended up with:

```sh
#!/bin/sh

set -e

readonly image="$1"
shift

readonly target="/var/lib/machines/$image"

readonly container="$( buildah from --pull=false --name "infra.$image" "$image:latest" )"
readonly mount="$( buildah mount "$container" )"
exec mount -o bind "$mount" "$target"
```

and a corresponding umount script:

```sh
#!/bin/sh

readonly image="$1"
shift

readonly target="/var/lib/machines/$image"

umount "$target"
buildah umount "infra.$image"
exec buildah rm "infra.$image"
```

that gets run as part of the `ExecStartPre` and `ExecStopPost` commands in the `.service` associated with each image's systemd service.
